author.name: think like a real estate appraiser

profile_bio.description: Follow me. Real estate appraisals, investing, house flipping, in-state rentals, out-of-state rentals, residential development, stocks - E-Book avail.

kept.text:
What if you could predict a home’s sale price almost perfectly… without ever stepping inside?

Seby Rubino reveals the dashboard of @resilabsai, a real estate super intelligence 

Every single day, ResiLabs runs a global competition.

AI engineers build pricing models, upload them to Hugging Face, and submit them.

Then ResiLabs tests each model:

How accurately can it predict what a house will actually sell for?

At the end of the day, the best-performing model gets selected, tested internally, and integrated into the product.

That means the system improves… every single day

Right now, the top model is hitting 98.67% accuracy

So when ResiLabs predicts a home’s price, it’s usually within about 1–2% of the actual sale price

Sometimes it misses slightly.

Sometimes it’s almost exact, down to a fraction of a percent.

And here’s the crazy part 

ResiLabs built this entire system for about $245,000

For comparison, @zillow spent roughly $25 million on its pricing system, Zestimate 

This product doesn’t just scale

It evolves, every day

profile_bio.description: iTrade | iClip ✂️

kept.text:
Things the internet thinks will replace Realtors:

→ Zillow (lost $881M on iBuying)
→ Redfin (cut staff twice in 18 months)
→ AI chatbots (can't open a lockbox)
→ FSBO sellers (sell for 18% less on average)
→ TikTok advice (from people who've never closed a deal)

Things that actually replace Realtors:

→ Other Realtors who work harder than them

kept.text:
Some crazy stuff is going on in Nashville's housing market.

Opendoor, America's largest homebuyer, just purchased this house for $462K in March.

But then immediately relisted and cut the price to $430k.

a $32,000 loss on price in a matter of weeks. 

On top of that, the appraised value for this house is $548,000.

Meaning today's list price is 22% below 2025 appraisal. 

Suggesting major downward pressure on values in Nashville. 

(that, or both Opendoor and the Nashville tax appraiser don't know what they're doing).
